prerequisites: A Microsoft work, school, or personal account with access to an Outlook mailbox.
Access to the Microsoft Entra admin center (Azure portal) to register an application and create a client secret.
An app registration that provides the Application (client) ID used as client_id.
A client secret created on the app registration, used as client_secret.
Delegated Microsoft Graph mail permissions (for example Mail.Read) plus the offline_access scope so a refresh token can be issued, used as refresh_token.
Optionally, your Microsoft Entra tenant ID (Directory ID) if you want to scope the connection to a specific tenant rather than the common endpoint (tenant_id).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow) on the Microsoft identity platform
authMethods.summary: Outlook is accessed through the Microsoft Graph mail API, which authenticates with the Microsoft identity platform (Microsoft Entra ID). The connector uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow on behalf of a user: a registered application supplies its Application (client) ID and a client secret, and after the user consents to the requested delegated permissions, Microsoft issues a refresh token (when the offline_access scope is granted) that the connector exchanges for access tokens. These three values populate the client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token fields.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: In the Microsoft Entra admin center (Azure portal), register a new application. After registration, copy the Application (client) ID; this is the client_id value. See the Microsoft Graph app-registration guide for the exact steps.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: On the app registration, go to Certificates & secrets and create a new client secret. Copy the secret value immediately (it is shown only once); this is the client_secret value.

setupSteps.title: Add Microsoft Graph mail permissions and offline_access
setupSteps.body: Under API permissions, add the delegated Microsoft Graph permissions your sync requires (for example Mail.Read) along with offline_access so a refresh token can be returned. Grant consent as needed for your organization.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a refresh token via the authorization code flow
setupSteps.body: Complete the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow on behalf of a user so the user consents and the Microsoft identity platform returns a refresh token. Provide that token as the refresh_token value.

setupSteps.title: Set the tenant (optional)
setupSteps.body: If you want to restrict authentication to a single Microsoft Entra tenant rather than the common/shared endpoint, provide your tenant's Directory (tenant) ID as the optional tenant_id value. Leave it unset to use the default endpoint.
